 {"id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","title":"Verification Gate: cross-epic verification toolchain contract (unit + e2e + logging audit)","description":"## Objective
Final acceptance gate for the shared verification contract used across all hardening epics.

## Required Validation
1. Unit tests for scenario-ID, log-schema, and artifact-manifest validators.
2. End-to-end dry runs across representative epics proving runner/result-schema compliance.
3. Structured-log audit proving required correlation fields are present and validated.

## Gate Commands
- rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets
- rch exec -- cargo test -p ffs-harness -- --nocapture
- cross-epic E2E dry-run pack with manifest/result-schema validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shared verification contract is executable and enforced.
- All epic verification gates are wired to this contract gate.
- Contract drift is automatically detected before release acceptance.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.256817517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T14:18:41.214964113Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T14:18:41.214268861Z","close_reason":"Final acceptance gate for verification contract: (1) Extended verification_runner.rs with validate_scenario_ids(), validate_gate_contract(), structured logging with operation_id/scenario_id/outcome correlation fields — 26 unit tests. (2) Created ffs_verification_gate_e2e.sh with 9 scenarios covering: artifact_manifest tests (36), log_contract tests (17), verification_runner tests (26), scenario catalog validation, cross-epic script conformance, logging field audit, schema version consistency, marker format sync, full harness suite (204 tests). Clippy clean, all 204 ffs-harness tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_verification_contract","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.256817517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.414570202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.570336006Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.724217238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.886155847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"bd-hei34","title":"add mounted-path ext4 SEEK_DATA/SEEK_HOLE edge-case coverage for fully-allocated and leading-hole files","description":"Survey finding: fuse_e2e currently verifies ext4 SEEK_DATA/SEEK_HOLE only for a middle punched-hole layout, while ffs-core also implements fully-allocated and leading-hole semantics. Scope: add mounted-path ext4 probes that freeze SEEK_HOLE on a fully allocated file returning the virtual EOF hole, and SEEK_DATA/SEEK_HOLE on a file with a leading sparse hole returning the next data offset and hole start correctly, including EOF ENXIO behavior. Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E covers these two additional sparse-layout edge cases with exact offsets.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:10:32.731277659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:10:32.731277659Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-hei34","title":"add mounted-path ext4 SEEK_DATA/SEEK_HOLE edge-case coverage for fully-allocated and leading-hole files","description":"Survey finding: fuse_e2e currently verifies ext4 SEEK_DATA/SEEK_HOLE only for a middle punched-hole layout, while ffs-core also implements fully-allocated and leading-hole semantics. Scope: add mounted-path ext4 probes that freeze SEEK_HOLE on a fully allocated file returning the virtual EOF hole, and SEEK_DATA/SEEK_HOLE on a file with a leading sparse hole returning the next data offset and hole start correctly, including EOF ENXIO behavior. Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E covers these two additional sparse-layout edge cases with exact offsets.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:10:32.731277659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:47:30.900657933Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:47:30.898786171Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-hks","title":"ffs-block: Plan write-back cache + dirty tracking (phased)","description":"Future-facing task: define how cached writes become durable.

Deliverables:
- DirtyTracker design: which blocks are dirty, ordering constraints, flush scheduling.
- Interaction with MVCC: versioned writes vs overwriting.
- Failure/cancellation semantics (fsync, flush on close, etc).

Acceptance:
- A concrete design exists in docs/spec before any write-back code is added.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:07.481262948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:48:46.342359407Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:48:46.342335122Z","close_reason":"Design doc created at docs/design-writeback-cache.md with 3-phase plan: DirtyTracker, FlushDaemon, MVCC integration","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hks","depends_on_id":"bd-220","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:27.218279466Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hks","depends_on_id":"bd-5iz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:27.135355880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hm8z","title":"Add OpenFs xattr round-trip integration coverage on writable fixtures","description":"Add cross-crate integration coverage in crates/ffs-core/tests/cross_crate_integration.rs for public OpenFs xattr mutation APIs on writable fixtures. Prefer low-conflict test-only work; verify setxattr/getxattr/listxattr/removexattr behavior, ideally covering btrfs and ext4 where fixture support allows.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:12:44.027001420Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:19:55.024475630Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T20:19:55.024274880Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -159,7 +159,7 @@ behavior of that operation, not whether the row is unimplemented.
 | FUSE readdir | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::readdir` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE read | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::read` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE readlink | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::readlink` via `OpenFs` |
-| FUSE mount runtime | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | Production runtime lifecycle, signal handling, dispatch coverage, and CI-safe skip behavior are implemented; OQ4 (`bd-h6nz.6.4`) is resolved for V1.x with explicit `flush` (non-durable) vs `fsync`/`fsyncdir` (durability boundary) contract and writeback-cache-disabled policy. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now emits `SCENARIO_RESULT` coverage for ext4 (`ext4_rw_flush`, `ext4_rw_fdatasync`, `ext4_rw_fsync`, `ext4_rw_fsyncdir`, `ext4_rw_seek_data_hole`) and btrfs (`btrfs_rw_flush`, `btrfs_rw_fdatasync`, `btrfs_rw_fsync`, `btrfs_rw_fsyncdir`, `btrfs_rw_seek_data_hole`) through real file flushes, file- and directory-FD sync calls, and punched-hole `SEEK_DATA`/`SEEK_HOLE` probes over FUSE with EOF `ENXIO` verification; btrfs read-only mounted-path `fsync`, `fdatasync`, and `fsyncdir` now also freeze exact `EROFS` with no data or dirent drift. |
+| FUSE mount runtime | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | Production runtime lifecycle, signal handling, dispatch coverage, and CI-safe skip behavior are implemented; OQ4 (`bd-h6nz.6.4`) is resolved for V1.x with explicit `flush` (non-durable) vs `fsync`/`fsyncdir` (durability boundary) contract and writeback-cache-disabled policy. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now emits `SCENARIO_RESULT` coverage for ext4 (`ext4_rw_flush`, `ext4_rw_fdatasync`, `ext4_rw_fsync`, `ext4_rw_fsyncdir`, `ext4_rw_seek_data_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `ext4_rw_seek_leading_hole`) and btrfs (`btrfs_rw_flush`, `btrfs_rw_fdatasync`, `btrfs_rw_fsync`, `btrfs_rw_fsyncdir`, `btrfs_rw_seek_data_hole`) through real file flushes, file- and directory-FD sync calls, and mounted-path `SEEK_DATA`/`SEEK_HOLE` probes for punched-hole, fully allocated, and leading-hole layouts with EOF `ENXIO` verification; btrfs read-only mounted-path `fsync`, `fdatasync`, and `fsyncdir` now also freeze exact `EROFS` with no data or dirent drift. |
 | FUSE ioctl FIEMAP | `include/linux/fiemap.h` | ✅ | `FsOps::fiemap` queries ext4 extent tree via `collect_extents_with_scope` and btrfs extent data items via `btrfs_fiemap_extent_items`. FUSE `ioctl` handler parses `FS_IOC_FIEMAP` (0xC020660B), rejects unsupported request flags with `EBADR`, honors `FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC` by fsyncing writable mounts before extent lookup, and marshals the fiemap header plus extent array. `crates/ffs-core` now has deterministic btrfs coverage for inline extents (`physical=0`, `FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST`) and keep-size prealloc extents (`FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN`), while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` verifies mounted-path FIEMAP reporting for ext4 regular files, ext4 `SYNC` and invalid-request-flag contracts, plus btrfs regular, inline, and keep-size prealloc files. Note: FUSE transport may not deliver this ioctl to userspace on all kernel versions. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::get_inode_flags` returns raw ext4 `i_flags` field. FUSE `ioctl` handler dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS` (0x80086601). Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path `GETFLAGS` on a writable ext4 file. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION / EXT4_IOC_SETVERSION | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::get_inode_generation` returns ext4 `i_generation`, and `FsOps::set_inode_generation` now updates that field in-place, persists it via `ffs_inode::write_inode`, and keeps the ext4-only contract explicit. `ffs-fuse` dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION` (0x80086603) and `EXT4_IOC_SETVERSION` (0x40086604), enforces the 4-byte userspace payload contract, and commits `SETVERSION` through the normal write request scope. `crates/ffs-core` unit tests cover direct ext4 mutation plus reopen persistence, `crates/ffs-fuse` unit tests cover getter encoding and setter routing, and `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now proves mounted-path `SETVERSION`/`GETVERSION` roundtrips when the current kernel/FUSE stack forwards the ioctl. The mounted-path test soft-skips with explicit scenario results when the kernel/VFS rejects `GETVERSION` or `SETVERSION` before userspace dispatch. |
